─[ Contribution and Sync Hardening ]─ ┐

Contribution and Sync Hardening

Goal

Support high-trust experimental direct-to-main workflows while preserving a production path that is PR-first, conflict-safe, and resilient to operator error.

This plan owns the pilot-readiness safety tranche before multi-tier pilot execution begins.

Context

Pilot testing needs speed, and direct mode is useful in controlled environments. At the same time, parent-level contribution workflows and sync behavior must be hardened before broader organizational rollout.

Fourth Assessment Findings (2026-02-11)

The deep-dive sync system analysis identified concrete safety gaps:

  1. ✅ Direct-mode rebase/fetch failures were not enforced: fixed with explicit fetch/rebase exit checking and failure handling before push.
  2. ✅ No symlink sanitization: fixed by rejecting symlinks in contribution paths.
  3. ✅ Empty ledger causes mass overwrite risk: fixed with explicit empty-ledger warning before high-risk sync.
  4. ✅ No concurrent sync protection: fixed with ledger lockfile enforcement and fail-fast behavior.
  5. No “keep mine forever” exclusion: If a local file is intentionally different from parent, every sync triggers a conflict. No way to say “stop syncing this file.”
  6. Conflict resolution has no merge option: Only keep-local or take-parent. At scale (50+ instances with local modifications), manual resolution becomes a bottleneck.
  7. Batch resolution has no rollback: --accept-local-all on a corrupted ledger keeps hundreds of stale files with no undo.

Scope

1. Direct mode safety (experimental)

  • Keep --direct explicitly gated behind LORE_ALLOW_DIRECT_PUSH=1
  • Add explicit preflight checks for direct mode:
    • clean or intentionally staged working tree
    • parent reachability
    • branch divergence handling policy
  • Add clear rollback instructions in command output and docs
  • Require structured pilot notes for each direct-mode use (why direct, what changed, outcome)

2. PR-first hardening (default path)

  • Strengthen contribution metadata for maintainer decisions
  • Improve failure messages and recovery guidance for failed push/PR creation
  • Ensure branch naming, commit metadata, and merge guidance are consistent across team/org contribution scripts
  • Validate that agent artifacts remain non-contributable and are regenerated locally
  • Create contribution PR decision template (.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/contribution.md or similar) that guides the receiving maintainer: Accept as-is? Promote (rename prefix)? Request changes? Reject? Include naming rationale field so promotion decisions are documented in the PR itself

3. Path and content safety

  • Harden contribution path normalization and validation
  • Add symlink sanitization: Reject symlinks in contributed paths ([[ -L "$path" ]] check before copy)
  • Block ambiguous broad paths by default (for example, root-level blanket contributions)
  • Expand secret scanning coverage and test vectors while keeping false-positive rates manageable
  • Add test cases for traversal-like or malformed path inputs
  • Add E2E test for adversarial contribution (symlink, path traversal, broad directory)

4. Sync conflict ergonomics

  • Add risk-tiered guidance for --into-branch (recommend by default for high-change syncs)
  • Improve conflict listing and batch-resolution messaging
  • Add stronger resolution playbooks for mixed local/parent edits
  • Ensure local changes are never silently overwritten outside explicit force semantics
  • Add empty ledger detection: Warn if ledger exists but tracks zero files when repo has 10+ expected sync targets
  • Add ledger file locking: Create .lore-sync-state.lock during sync operations, fail fast on concurrent access
  • Evaluate per-file sync exclusion: Allow operators to mark files as “keep local, stop syncing” (.lore-sync-ignore or similar)
  • Add batch resolution rollback: Snapshot pre-resolution state before --accept-local-all / --accept-parent-all
  • Add structured sync change preview: Before syncing, output a semantic summary grouped by category (new skills, modified docs, removed files, env knowledge changes) — not just raw file-level --dry-run output. Helps operators understand what’s incoming before committing to a sync

5. Adversarial testing (pilot readiness)

  • Add E2E test: two instances create conflicting content, both contribute, verify detection and resolution
  • Add E2E test: sync with empty ledger, verify warning before mass overwrite
  • Add E2E test: partial sync failure (simulate disk full or network drop), verify recovery path
  • Add E2E test: concurrent sync to same repo, verify ledger integrity

6. Version pinning strategy (multi-tier)

parent.pin only pins the immediate parent. In a 3-tier setup (org → team → instance), inconsistent pinning across tiers causes version mismatches: Org changes, Team pins old Org, Instance syncs Team — gets stale Org content.

  • Document recommended pinning practices for 2-tier and 3-tier setups in docs/guides/sync-troubleshooting.md
  • Evaluate whether sync scripts should warn when a child pins a parent that itself has a stale pin (parent’s pin is behind its own parent’s HEAD)
  • Evaluate transitive pin awareness: should sync-from-team report that Team’s org pin is N commits behind Org HEAD?
  • Define guidance: when should operators pin (stability) vs float (freshness)?
  • Add troubleshooting section for version mismatch symptoms and recovery

Implementation Order

Phase 0 Pilot-Readiness Mandatory

  1. ✅ Path validation hardening + tests (including symlink sanitization and traversal/root rejection)
  2. ✅ Direct mode rebase/fetch enforcement and audit logging
  3. ✅ Empty-ledger detection warning and structured sync preview summary
  4. ✅ Adversarial E2E additions (malformed/empty ledger, lock contention, path abuse)

Post-Pilot Follow-on

  1. PR metadata and maintainer decision template consistency pass
  2. Conflict ergonomics updates and docs alignment
  3. Evaluate per-file sync exclusion and batch-resolution rollback model

Success Criteria

  • Direct mode remains opt-in and auditable in pilot logs
  • PR-first path is consistently reliable for instance-to-team and team-to-org contributions
  • Contribution scripts reject malformed and traversal/symlink paths with clear errors
  • Conflict workflows remain data-safe and easier to resolve under pilot pressure
  • Test suite includes new safety edge cases and passes in CI
  • Phase 0 pilot-readiness mandatory tranche is complete before pilot kickoff

Related

  • Live Test Protocol
  • Evidence and Validation
  • Sync Troubleshooting (archived in lorehq-legacy)
